summary:
Tympanometry assesses ear canal volume and middle ear pressures while providing information about eardrum mobility; VEMP tests auditory nerves rather than Eustachian tube functionality; Rhinomanometry measures nasal air flow which has no relation to Eustachian tubes; the Politzer test involves using a pneumatic otoscope to evaluate how well the eardrum moves when subjected to positive pressure, indicating appropriate opening of the Eustachian tube. Thus, the Politzer test effectively determines Eustachian tube function through direct observation under specific conditions.\(\boxed{D}\)
```

Thus, the Politzer test effectively determines Eustachian tube function through direct observation under specific conditions.\(\boxed{D}\)
```. Answer choice: D
